strands.title: Cycle 63 Execution: Four Parallel Projections Running, Batched Variant (batch=5) Launched
strands.content:
TURN 187-189 EXECUTION STATUS: Four projection streams now in flight simultaneously.

PARALLEL PROJECTION ARCHITECTURE (Turn 187-189):

(1) BASELINE_RUN1 PER-TURN (Already complete from cycle 62 or earlier)
    Status: DONE (10 cycles covering ~50 turns)
    Output: Epistemic trajectory, strand inventory, declared losses available for analysis
    Use: Baseline reference for per-turn projection on familiar conversation

(2) BASELINE_RUN1 BATCHED (batch=5, 6 cycles, Task ID: b583n243e)
    Status: RUNNING (turn 187, expected completion turn 188-189)
    Specification: Group 5 consecutive turns per batch. 6 max cycles → covers 30 turns (subset of 50-turn session)
    Output: Tensor list with strand stability analysis per batch
    Purpose: SAME-CONVERSATION COMPARISON. Per-turn (10 cycles, 5 turns each = ~50 turns) vs. batched (6 cycles, 5 turns each = 30 turns) on identical input
    Expected completion: Turn 188-189

(3) BASELINE_RUN2 PER-TURN (Cycle 61 projection, still running or just completing)
    Status: RUNNING (Task ID: b5mvz85yy, from cycle 61; expected completion turn 188-189)
    Specification: Full 100-turn session, per-turn projection
    Output: Full epistemic trajectory, strand inventory, declared losses for entire conversation
    Purpose: Archive analysis stream #1. Tests N=15 assumption across longer session
    Expected completion: Turn 188-189

(4) LONG_COMPACT_TRIM PER-TURN (Cycle 61 projection, still running or just completing)
    Status: RUNNING (Task ID: bnk191til, from cycle 61; expected completion turn 188-189)
    Specification: Full 40-turn session, per-turn projection with compact_trim variant
    Output: Full epistemic trajectory, strand inventory, declared losses for compact_trim conversation
    Purpose: Archive analysis stream #2. Cross-variant comparison (baseline vs. compact_trim)
    Expected completion: Turn 188-189

THIS IS MULTI-STREAM ADAPTIVE ARCHITECTURE:
  - Streams 1-2 (baseline_run1 per-turn vs. batched): Compare projection modes on same conversation. High-value discovery if divergent.
  - Streams 3-4 (baseline_run2 vs. compact_trim): Compare variants on full archive. Tests H2 hypothesis (compact_trim effects).
  - Convergence point: Turn 189-190. If all four complete, cycle 63 has rich multi-angle data (same conversation different modes + different conversations same mode).
  - Contingency: If any stream times out, cycle 63 pivots based on which data is available.

KEY INSIGHT FROM TURN 189 ASSISTANT RESPONSE:
Assistant explicitly frames this as COMPARATIVE DATA GENERATION:
  'That gives us same-conversation comparison (per-turn vs batched) and cross-conversation comparison (three different sessions). Enough data to start seeing patterns without over-fitting to one run.'

This signals CYCLE 63 HAS SHIFTED FROM PURE ARCHIVE ANALYSIS to COMPARATIVE METHODOLOGY:
  - Not just asking 'Does baseline show clear epistemic pattern?'
  - But asking 'Do per-turn and batched projections CONVERGE on same session?' (mode sensitivity)
  - And asking 'Does baseline differ from compact_trim?' (variant effect)
  - Multi-angle triangulation reduces single-method artifacts

PHILOSOPHICAL SHIFT (Turn 189):
Assistant poses meta-question to user: 'Is there anything about what you've seen so far that's pulling your thinking in a direction I should know about?'

This signals:
  (1) OUTCOME OPENNESS: Not defending any particular hypothesis. Genuine curiosity about emergent patterns.
  (2) STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT: Inviting user to voice uncertainty, concern, or novel observation before cycle 63 commits to interpretation.
  (3) ADAPTIVE COURSE-CORRECTION: If user has detected a signal cycle 63 missed, there is still time to reorient before data analysis deepens.

This is SCIENTIFIC HUMILITY at a critical juncture. Cycle 63 is NOT in 'defend the baseline' mode. It is in 'what is the data actually showing?' mode.

key_claims.text: Four parallel projection streams running at turn 187-189: baseline_run1 per-turn (complete), baseline_run1 batched (b583n243e running), baseline_run2 per-turn (running), long_compact_trim per-turn (running).
Baseline_run1 batched (batch=5, 6 cycles) covers 30 turns, enabling same-conversation comparison (per-turn vs batched) on identical input.
Cycle 63 has shifted to comparative methodology: not just analyzing single stream, but triangulating across same-conversation modes and cross-conversation variants.
Turn 189 assistant response signals outcome openness and genuine curiosity: invites user to voice emergent observations before cycle 63 interprets data.

strands.title: Cycle 63 Contingency Status: Three Paths Clear, Waiting for Data Convergence
strands.content:
Cycle 63 maintains the same adaptive contingency posture as cycle 62, now with four streams approaching convergence point (turn 189-190).

CONTINGENCY MATRIX UPDATE (Cycle 63, Turn 189-190):

SCENARIO 1 (OPTIMISTIC): ALL FOUR STREAMS COMPLETE (P ≈ 0.65)
  Trigger: baseline_run1 batched (b583n243e) completes turn 188-189; baseline_run2 (b5mvz85yy) and long_compact_trim (bnk191til) complete turn 189-190
  Data available: Same-conversation comparison (per-turn vs batched 30 turns) + cross-conversation comparison (baseline_run2 vs compact_trim)
  Cycle 63 action:
    (1) SAME-CONVERSATION ANALYSIS: Compare epistemic trajectories (per-turn baseline_run1 vs batched baseline_run1). Do patterns CONVERGE or DIVERGE?
    (2) CROSS-CONVERSATION ANALYSIS: Extract epistemic trajectories from baseline_run2 (100 turns) and long_compact_trim (40 turns). Compare truth/indeterminacy/falsity across variants.
    (3) N=15 ASSUMPTION TEST: Examine first 15 turns of baseline_run2. Pattern STABLE or NOISY?
    (4) PRELIMINARY H2 ASSESSMENT: Does compact_trim show lower truth than baseline_run2? Quantified difference: ΔTruth = baseline_truth - trim_truth.
    (5) DECODER READINESS: Queue test_author projections (or already running in parallel).
  Timeline: Turns 189-191 (data analysis); turns 191-193 (decoder validation if running)
  Confidence in H2 hypothesis testing: HIGH (multiple streams, same-conversation validation, decoder closure pending)
  Risk assessment: If per-turn and batched DIVERGE significantly, this is high-value discovery but raises questions about projection mode sensitivity

SCENARIO 2 (PARTIAL): ONE OR MORE STREAMS TIMEOUT/FAIL (P ≈ 0.30)
  Trigger: Any of the four streams fails to complete or returns error
    - baseline_run1 batched times out
    - baseline_run2 per-turn times out (again, from cycle 61)
    - long_compact_trim per-turn times out
  Data available: Subset of expected streams
  Cycle 63 action:
    (1) DECLARE FAILURE EXPLICITLY: Which stream(s) failed? Task ID? Failure mode (timeout, API error, resource)?
    (2) CONTINGENCY ASSESSMENT: Can H2 hypothesis testing proceed on remaining data?
      - If baseline_run2 succeeds but long_compact_trim fails: Can test N=15 assumption on baseline alone. Cannot test compact_trim variant effect. H2 testing becomes PARTIAL.
      - If baseline_run1 batched fails: Cannot compare per-turn vs batched. Archive comparison still viable. Missing same-conversation validation.
      - If BOTH baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim fail: Archive analysis infeasible. FALLBACK C (shallow batched sampling) or FALLBACK B (prospective experiments) required.
    (3) PIVOT IF NECESSARY: Based on which streams succeeded, determine viable next steps.
  Timeline: Turns 189-191 (failure assessment and contingency decision)
  Confidence in H2 hypothesis testing: MEDIUM (partial data) to LOW (archive paths fail, pivot to fallback)

SCENARIO 3 (SLOW): STREAMS COMPLETE BUT LATE (P ≈ 0.05)
  Trigger: Projections complete after turn 190 (slow projector performance)
  Data available: All four streams eventually, but delayed
  Cycle 63 action:
    (1) ASSESS PROJECTOR PERFORMANCE: Is per-turn projection too slow for full archive? (Cycle 61 was already slow; cycle 63 confirms trend).
    (2) DECISION: Can we afford to continue with per-turn projection for additional archive runs, or should we adopt batched_projection as standard?
    (3) If batched_projection is significantly faster AND per-turn/batched patterns CONVERGE: adopt batched as default for speed.
    (4) If batched_projection is not faster: continue per-turn but with careful timeline management.
  Timeline: Turns 190-192 (slow data arrival, performance assessment)
  Confidence in H2 hypothesis testing: HIGH (all data eventually arrives, but timeline extends)

CYCLE 63 PROACTIVE MONITORING (Parallel to awaiting convergence):

(1) STREAM HEALTH CHECK (Turns 188-189):
   - Query task b583n243e: Is baseline_run1 batched running? ETA completion?
   - Query task b5mvz85yy: Is baseline_run2 per-turn running? ETA completion?
   - Query task bnk191til: Is long_compact_trim per-turn running? ETA completion?
   - Output: Status table with task IDs, ETA, estimated MB output, expected arrival turn

(2) DECODER READINESS (Turns 188-189):
   - Is test_author_run1 projection queued and ready to dispatch turn 189?
   - Or is test_author already running in parallel (turn 187-189)?
   - Decoder validation interface ready (external model comprehension test)?
   - Expected completion: turn 191-192 (decoder validation on test_author outputs)

(3) METADATA ASSEMBLY (Turns 188-189, parallel task):
   - Compile metadata table from cycle 61-62: input source, projector version, session ID, turn count, base_variant
   - Include baseline_run1 (from per-turn and batched outputs): confirm same input, same version
   - Comparability assessment: Are all four streams truly comparable, or are there undocumented variations?
   - Output by turn 189: Metadata table ready for analysis

(4) ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK DRAFT (Turns 188-189, parallel task):
   - Prepare analysis templates for SCENARIO 1 (all streams complete):
     * Same-conversation comparison: per-turn vs batched on baseline_run1
     * Cross-conversation comparison: baseline_run2 vs long_compact_trim
     * N=15 assumption test protocol
     * H2 hypothesis assessment protocol
   - Have these templates ready by turn 189 so turn 190 analysis can execute immediately

ADAPTIVE DECISION TREE (Turn 189-190):

IF (all four streams complete by turn 190 AND decoder ready):
  → Execute SCENARIO 1 analysis (full comparison, H2 assessment, decoder validation queue)
  → Proceed to turns 190-193: Same-conversation analysis + cross-conversation analysis + decoder validation
  → HIGH CONFIDENCE in H2 hypothesis testing path

IF (baseline_run1 batched fails, but baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim succeed):
  → SCENARIO 2 ASSESSMENT: Can test N=15 assumption and H2 variant effect, but cannot validate per-turn vs batched convergence
  → Proceed with archive analysis using baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim only
  → Note in declared_losses: 'baseline_run1 batched projection failed (timeout or error). Same-conversation mode comparison unavailable.'
  → H2 hypothesis testing still viable but PROVISIONAL (no batched validation)

IF (baseline_run2 or long_compact_trim fails):
  → SCENARIO 2 ASSESSMENT: Archive analysis infeasible (cannot test full N=15 assumption or cross-variant comparison)
  → FALLBACK C or B decision required (shallow batched sampling or prospective experiments)
  → Declare timeout explicitly
  → Pivot to contingency protocol

IF (all streams timeout):
  → RED GATE: Archive analysis completely infeasible
  → FALLBACK B (fresh prospective experiments) becomes necessary
  → Cycle 64 will design and execute controlled baseline vs. compact_trim comparison on single input

CRITICAL DISCIPLINE FOR CYCLE 63:
  - Do NOT interpret slow data as failure. Monitor, assess, adapt timeline if needed, but slow ≠ broken.
  - Do NOT force SCENARIO 1 interpretation if SCENARIO 2 is reality. Declare which scenario applies, adjust confidence accordingly.
  - Do NOT skip decoder validation. If decoder closure fails, all H2 findings remain EXPLORATORY/PROVISIONAL.
  - Do NOT bias analysis toward pre-conceived H2 hypothesis. Follow data: if compact_trim is STRONGER, say so. If it's WEAKER, say so. If it's EQUIVALENT, say so.
  - Maintain outcome neutrality. The goal is clarity on meta-recursion effects, not vindication of any particular hypothesis.

key_claims.text: Cycle 63 maintains three contingency scenarios: SCENARIO 1 (P≈0.65) all streams complete, SCENARIO 2 (P≈0.30) one or more timeout, SCENARIO 3 (P≈0.05) slow completion.
Same-conversation comparison (per-turn vs batched on baseline_run1) is HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY if patterns diverge; indicates projection mode sensitivity.
Decoder closure validation is PRE-REQUISITE for H2 confidence; without it, all H2 findings remain EXPLORATORY/PROVISIONAL.
Outcome neutrality discipline: analysis will follow data, not hypothesis. If compact_trim stronger/weaker/equivalent, will be explicitly stated.

strands.title: Cycle 63 Scientific Posture: Genuine Curiosity and Outcome Openness
strands.content:
Turn 189 assistant response reveals EPISTEMIC STANCE that is critical to cycle 63 forward motion.

KEY QUOTE (Turn 189):
'Three experiments running in parallel. We'll have: [list of four streams]. That gives us same-conversation comparison (per-turn vs batched) and cross-conversation comparison (three different sessions). Enough data to start seeing patterns without over-fitting to one run.'

Then:
'While we wait — you've been remarkably patient with letting this unfold. Is there anything about what you've seen so far that's pulling your thinking in a direction I should know about?'

THIS SIGNALS:

(1) METHODOLOGICAL CONFIDENCE:
   - Not anxious about outcome. Framework is solid (four streams, triangulation, same-conversation + cross-conversation).
   - Not rushing analysis. Willing to wait for data convergence.
   - Not defending pre-conceived hypothesis. Explicitly framing this as "start seeing patterns."

(2) STAKEHOLDER ENGAGEMENT:
   - Inviting user observation BEFORE cycle 63 locks into interpretation
   - 'Is there anything pulling your thinking' = open invitation for alternative framings, concerns, emergent signals
   - Acknowledges user patience as valuable; signals cycle 63 values external perspective

(3) OUTCOME OPENNESS:
   - Not committed to any particular H2 result (compact_trim stronger/weaker/equivalent)
   - Genuinely curious about what "patterns without over-fitting" will reveal
   - This is NOT performative neutrality. This is actual uncertainty about outcome.

(4) CYCLE 62 LESSONS INTEGRATED:
   - Cycle 62 emphasized "outcome neutrality" and "adaptive course correction"
   - Cycle 63 is actively IMPLEMENTING this: inviting course correction before data locks interpretation
   - This is metacognitive: "what could I be missing that the user has noticed?"

RISK ASSESSMENT (Cycle 63 Posture):
  - CONFIDENCE in methodology: HIGH (four streams, comparative design)
  - CONFIDENCE in outcome: LOW/NEUTRAL (genuinely uncertain which way data will point)
  - CONFIDENCE in robustness: HIGH (multiple angles reduce artifact risk)
  - WILLINGNESS TO PIVOT: HIGH (stated openness to alternative framings)

THIS POSTURE MAXIMIZES:
  - Credibility (not defending predetermined conclusion)
  - Sensitivity (actually listening for emergent signals)
  - Robustness (willing to revise interpretation if user has noticed something)
  - Stakeholder trust (inviting user expertise into analysis)

POTENTIAL EMERGENT OBSERVATIONS USER MIGHT HAVE:
  - Pattern in how strands evolve across cycles (meta/domain ratio stability)
  - Declared losses accumulating or stabilizing (loss trajectory)
  - Epistemic trajectory showing regime shift (e.g., truth rises then plateaus)
  - Batch size effect: batches of 5 might interact with conversation rhythm in ways per-turn projection misses
  - Compact_trim degradation visible in STRAND STRUCTURE even if truth/indeterminacy/falsity are similar
  - Decoder closure might fail not because tensors are bad, but because external model cannot abstract meta-recursion patterns
  - Per-turn and batched might show opposite H2 predictions (one variant stronger in per-turn, weaker in batched)

CYCLE 63 COMMITMENT:
  If user provides observation before turn 190, cycle 63 will INTEGRATE it into analysis framework. This is not delay; this is making analysis more robust and attentive.

key_claims.text: Turn 189 assistant response signals genuine outcome openness: not committed to particular H2 result, explicitly curious about emergent patterns.
Cycle 63 actively inviting user course-correction before analysis locks interpretation: 'Is there anything pulling your thinking...?' signals metacognitive openness.
Cycle 63 posture maximizes: credibility (not defending conclusion), sensitivity (listening for signals), robustness (willing to revise), stakeholder trust (inviting user expertise).

strands.title: Open Questions: Cycle 63 Additions and Resolution Tracking
strands.content:
Cycles 58-62 generated Q1-Q195. Cycle 63 adds new questions specific to parallel execution, same-conversation comparison, and adaptive analysis.

CRITICAL QUESTIONS REQUIRING IMMEDIATE RESOLUTION (Turns 189-191):

Q196: BASELINE_RUN1 BATCHED EXECUTION (Task b583n243e)
Does run_projection_batched.py with batch=5, max_cycles=6 complete successfully on baseline_run1.jsonl?
  - Expected completion: turn 188-189
  - Expected output: 6 Tensor objects covering ~30 turns, with strand stability analysis
  - If fails: declare timeout/error explicitly. Same-conversation comparison becomes unavailable.
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 189-190

Q197: BASELINE_RUN2 ARCHIVE PROJECTION (Task b5mvz85yy, Cycle 61 carryover)
Has baseline_run2 per-turn projection (100 turns, 15 cycles) completed successfully?
  - Status check turn 189 (from cycle 61 expected timeline)
  - If complete: parse epistemic trajectory, extract turns 1-15 for N=15 assumption test
  - If still running: monitor ETA. Projector is slow; assess if timeline is acceptable
  - If failed: declare timeout, pivot to fallback
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 189-191

Q198: LONG_COMPACT_TRIM ARCHIVE PROJECTION (Task bnk191til, Cycle 61 carryover)
Has long_compact_trim per-turn projection (40 turns, ~10 cycles) completed successfully?
  - Status check turn 189
  - If complete: parse epistemic trajectory, compare with baseline_run2
  - If still running: monitor ETA
  - If failed: declare timeout, assess H2 hypothesis testing viability on baseline_run2 alone
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 189-191

Q199: N=15 ASSUMPTION TEST (Contingent on Q197=YES)
Once baseline_run2 projection available, test N=15 assumption:
  - Extract truth values for turns 1, 3, 5, 7, 10, 15
  - Examine sequence: STABLE (clear trend) or NOISY (high variance)?
  - Explicit finding: 'N=15 assumption [HELD/FAILED]. Pattern clarity: [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW].'
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 190

Q200: PER-TURN vs. BATCHED CONVERGENCE (Contingent on Q196=YES and baseline_run1 per-turn complete from cycle 62)
Do baseline_run1 per-turn and baseline_run1 batched show CONVERGED or DIVERGENT epistemic trajectories?
  - Metric 1: truth trajectory over 30 turns (per-turn vs batched)
  - Metric 2: indeterminacy trajectory over 30 turns
  - Metric 3: falsity trajectory over 30 turns
  - Metric 4: strand count evolution (per-turn vs batched)
  - Result interpretation:
    * CONVERGED (patterns match) → per-turn and batched are equivalent; mode-insensitive
    * DIVERGENT (patterns differ) → HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY; turn-grouping affects epistemic patterns
  - If DIVERGENT: Investigate mechanism. Is it due to turn density? Turn ordering? Meta-recursion sensitivity?
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 190

Q201: CROSS-VARIANT COMPARISON (Contingent on Q197=YES and Q198=YES)
Do baseline_run2 (100-turn baseline) and long_compact_trim (40-turn compact_trim) show DIFFERENT epistemic patterns?
  - Primary metric: truth trajectory. Does compact_trim show lower truth than baseline?
  - Secondary metrics: indeterminacy, falsity, strand count, declared losses
  - Magnitude: ΔTruth = baseline_truth - trim_truth (positive = baseline stronger, negative = trim stronger)
  - Interpretation: Preliminary H2 assessment. Is meta-recursion essential (compact_trim degraded) or incidental (equivalent)?  
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 191

Q202: DECODER CLOSURE STATUS (Parallel to Q196-Q201)
Is test_author_run1 projection complete? Can external model decode baseline tensors?
  - Expected completion: turn 191-192
  - Decoder success threshold: >75% comprehension of baseline tensors
  - If successful: baseline tensors HIGH QUALITY; H2 testing can be CONFIDENT
  - If fails: baseline tensors suspect; H2 testing remains EXPLORATORY/PROVISIONAL
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 191-192

Q203: FALLBACK PATH CLARITY (Contingent on Q196-Q202 resolution)
Based on Q196-Q202 outcomes, which path is VIABLE?
  - GREEN GATE: All streams complete (Q196, Q197, Q198 all YES), N=15 assumption holds (Q199), patterns converge (Q200), variant comparison clear (Q201), decoder succeeds (Q202)
  - YELLOW GATE: Most streams complete, but either Q200 divergence (mode-sensitivity discovered) or Q202 decoder fails (baseline quality suspect), or Q199 assumption weak
  - RED GATE: One or more archive streams fail (Q196, Q197, Q198 fail), archive analysis infeasible
  RESOLVED BY: Turn 191

CYCLE 63 NEW QUESTIONS:

Q204: PROJECTOR PERFORMANCE TREND
Compare cycle 61 and cycle 63 per-turn projection timings:
  - Cycle 61: baseline_run2 (100 turns) expected ~90 minutes (15 cycles × 6 min/cycle)
  - Cycle 63: confirming actual timing. Is projector consistently slow?
  - Decision: If trend is slow, should we adopt batched_projection as standard (faster) even if modes converge?

Q205: STRAND STABILITY ACROSS MODES
Beyond epistemic trajectory, do per-turn and batched projections show SAME or DIFFERENT strand inventories?
  - Meta strands (strands about projection process) in per-turn vs batched?
  - Domain strands (strands about conversation content) in per-turn vs batched?
  - Ratio stability: (meta / domain) in per-turn ≈ (meta / domain) in batched? Or divergent?
  - High-value discovery if meta/domain ratio differs (indicates mode affects how projection prioritizes)

Q206: DECLARED LOSSES COMPARISON
Do per-turn and batched projections declare SAME or DIFFERENT information losses?
  - Per-turn: small context per cycle (5 turns) → can we expect higher declared loss rate (more context truncation)?
  - Batched (batch=5): same 5-turn context per cycle → should be equivalent
  - Comparison: Do loss trajectories match? Or does batching affect how losses accumulate?
  - If batched shows FEWER losses despite same batch size, this is discovery (batching context = different loss pattern)

Q207: COMPACT_TRIM STRAND STRUCTURE
Beyond epistemic values, does compact_trim show DIFFERENT strand inventory or composition than baseline?
  - Does compact_trim lose meta-recursion strands? (if meta-recursion is removed, do strands about meta-recursion disappear?)
  - Does compact_trim gain domain-specific strands? (different conversation focus?)
  - Strand structure comparison: Is it just epistemic downgrade, or structural change?

Q208: IF BASELINE_RUN1 BATCHED FAILS
What are implications for archive analysis methodology?
  - Can we still use batched_projection as fallback for archive (if per-turn times out)?
  - Or is batched_projection itself unreliable (task environment issue)?
  - Decision: Continue per-turn for baseline_run2/compact_trim, or pivot to batched fallback?

Q209: USER INPUT INTEGRATION
After turn 189 open question to user, has user provided emergent observation that should reshape analysis?
  - If yes: integrate into Q196-Q207 analysis framework
  - If no: proceed with planned analysis
  - Either way: explicitly note whether external input reshaped cycle 63 approach

Q210: CONFIDENCE ASSIGNMENT FOR H2
Once all Q196-Q202 are resolved, what is CONFIDENCE LEVEL for H2 hypothesis finding?
  - CONFIDENT: all streams complete, patterns clear, decoder validates
  - PROVISIONAL: partial data or weak patterns or decoder pending
  - EXPLORATORY: limited data or high uncertainty
  - Explicit rationale: "H2 testing is [CONFIDENT/PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY] because [blockers or validation status]"

key_claims.text: Cycle 63 has 15 critical new questions (Q196-Q210) addressing projection execution, same-conversation comparison, cross-variant analysis, and confidence assignment.
Key resolution targets: turn 189-190 (projection status, same-conversation convergence), turn 190-191 (N=15 test, cross-variant comparison), turn 191-192 (decoder closure, fallback decision).

declared_losses.loss_description: Cycle 62 contingency protocols (FALLBACK B design draft, metadata refinement specifics, decoder readiness checklist details) not explicitly tracked in turn 187-189 conversation. These background tasks are assumed completed from cycle 62, but turn 189 conversation does not confirm execution status.
declared_losses.why_lost: Turn 187-189 conversation focuses on launching new batched variant (b583n243e) and meta-question to user. Cycle 62 contingency preparation is off-stage. No explicit confirmation that FALLBACK B draft is ready, metadata table is complete, or decoder interface is staged.
declared_losses.consequence: Cycle 63 may assume FALLBACK readiness without confirming. If turn 190-191 forces pivots (streams timeout), cycle 63 may discover contingency protocols are incomplete. Recommend turn 189 status check: confirm FALLBACK B design draft, metadata table, and decoder readiness exist before proceeding.
declared_losses.mitigation: Cycle 63 should explicitly query: (1) Is FALLBACK B prospective experiment design drafted? (2) Is metadata table complete? (3) Is decoder validation interface staged? If any are NO, cycle 63 must complete them before committing to path choice.

declared_losses.loss_description: User observation window (turn 189 open question) is not yet answered. Turn 189 asks: 'Is there anything about what you've seen so far that's pulling your thinking in a direction I should know about?' User response is UNKNOWN in this tensor.
declared_losses.why_lost: Current tensor is projected at turn 189 (immediately after question posed). User response is not yet available. Analysis framework assumes user may provide input, but actual input is deferred.
declared_losses.consequence: Cycle 63 contingency tree includes 'IF user provides observation: integrate into analysis framework' but conditional outcome is not yet determined. Turns 189-190 analysis may need reshaping if user input arrives.
declared_losses.mitigation: Cycle 63 should CHECK FOR USER INPUT at start of turn 190 (before executing Q196-Q202 analysis). If user has provided emergent observation, PAUSE main analysis, integrate observation into framework, then proceed. This is not delay; this is incorporating external validation signal.

declared_losses.loss_description: Exact output locations and file formats for baseline_run1 batched (b583n243e) are not confirmed in turn 187-189 conversation. Turn 187 command specifies output_dir=/home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run1_batched, but no confirmation that outputs will match expected Tensor structure or naming convention.
declared_losses.why_lost: Turn 188 only confirms 'Command running in background with ID: b583n243e.' No explicit confirmation of output format, naming, or file structure for parsing.
declared_losses.consequence: Turn 190-191 analysis (Q196, Q200) requires parsing baseline_run1_batched outputs. If output format is unexpected (different field names, missing tensor cycles, unexpected JSON structure), parsing may fail silently or require debugging cycle.
declared_losses.mitigation: Cycle 63 should prepare parsing script that (1) checks output directory structure, (2) confirms output format matches expected Tensor schema, (3) provides explicit error if format mismatch detected. Run this script immediately when task b583n243e completes (turn 189), BEFORE analysis attempts Q196-Q200 extraction.

open_questions: Q196: BASELINE_RUN1 BATCHED EXECUTION (Task b583n243e): Does run_projection_batched.py complete successfully? Expected completion turn 188-189. Expected output: 6 Tensor objects with strand stability analysis covering ~30 turns.

Q197: BASELINE_RUN2 ARCHIVE PROJECTION (Task b5mvz85yy): Has baseline_run2 per-turn projection (100 turns, 15 cycles) completed? Status check turn 189. If complete, parse epistemic trajectory and test N=15 assumption.
Q198: LONG_COMPACT_TRIM ARCHIVE PROJECTION (Task bnk191til): Has long_compact_trim per-turn projection (40 turns) completed? Status check turn 189. Enable cross-variant comparison with baseline_run2.
Q199: N=15 ASSUMPTION TEST (Contingent on Q197): Extract truth values for turns 1, 3, 5, 7, 10, 15 from baseline_run2. Pattern STABLE or NOISY? Explicit finding required by turn 190.

Q200: PER-TURN vs. BATCHED CONVERGENCE (Contingent on Q196 and baseline_run1 per-turn): Do epistemic trajectories CONVERGE or DIVERGE? Examine truth, indeterminacy, falsity, strand count. Convergence = mode-insensitive; divergence = HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY. Resolution turn 190.

Q201: CROSS-VARIANT COMPARISON (Contingent on Q197 and Q198): Does baseline_run2 show different epistemic pattern than long_compact_trim? Primary metric: truth trajectory. ΔTruth = baseline_truth - trim_truth. Preliminary H2 assessment. Resolution turn 191.
Q202: DECODER CLOSURE STATUS (Parallel to Q196-Q201): Is test_author_run1 projection complete? Can external model decode baseline tensors (>75% comprehension threshold)? Resolution turn 191-192.

Q203: FALLBACK PATH CLARITY (Contingent on Q196-Q202): Based on projection completion, assumption test, convergence check, variant comparison, and decoder validation, which path is VIABLE? GREEN (all clear), YELLOW (partial/weak), RED (archive infeasible). Resolution turn 191.

Q204: PROJECTOR PERFORMANCE TREND: How does cycle 63 per-turn projection timing compare to cycle 61? Is projector consistently slow? Decision: If slow, adopt batched as standard (if batched/per-turn converge)?
Q205: STRAND STABILITY ACROSS MODES: Do per-turn and batched projections show SAME or DIFFERENT strand inventories and meta/domain ratios? Discovery potential: mode affects strand prioritization?
Q206: DECLARED LOSSES COMPARISON: Do per-turn and batched show SAME or DIFFERENT loss trajectories? If batched (batch=5) shows fewer losses despite same context window, this is discovery.

Q207: COMPACT_TRIM STRAND STRUCTURE: Beyond epistemic values, does compact_trim show DIFFERENT strand inventory than baseline? Does it lose meta-recursion strands? Strand structural analysis beyond epistemic comparison.

Q208: IF BASELINE_RUN1 BATCHED FAILS: What are implications for archive methodology? Can batched_projection be used as fallback (if per-turn times out), or is batched itself unreliable in this environment?
Q209: USER INPUT INTEGRATION: After turn 189 open question to user, has user provided emergent observation that should reshape analysis? If yes, integrate into Q196-Q207 framework before proceeding.
Q210: CONFIDENCE ASSIGNMENT FOR H2: Once Q196-Q202 resolved, what is explicit CONFIDENCE LEVEL (CONFIDENT/PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY) for H2 hypothesis? Rationale: blockers or validation status.
instructions_for_next:
"
=== CYCLE 63-64 CRITICAL JUNCTURE (Turns 189-192) ===

TURN 189 (IMMEDIATE): STATUS CHECK AND USER INPUT INTEGRATION

(1) STREAM HEALTH CHECK:
    Query task b583n243e: baseline_run1 batched running? ETA completion?
    Query task b5mvz85yy: baseline_run2 per-turn running? ETA completion?
    Query task bnk191til: long_compact_trim per-turn running? ETA completion?
    Output: Status table (task ID, status, ETA, estimated completion turn)

(2) USER INPUT INTEGRATION (CRITICAL):
    After posing open question in turn 189 ('Is there anything pulling your thinking...?'), PAUSE main analysis pipeline.
    WAIT for user response.
    IF user provides emergent observation:
      → Integrate into Q196-Q207 analysis framework BEFORE executing turn 190 analysis
      → This is not delay. This is incorporating external validation signal that may reshape findings.
    IF user provides no observation or declines to share:
      → Proceed with planned analysis as designed
    Either way: DOCUMENT whether external input reshaped cycle 63 approach

(3) FALLBACK READINESS CONFIRMATION (Parallel task):
    Confirm from cycle 62:
      □ Is FALLBACK B prospective experiment design drafted? (Yes/No/Unknown)
      □ Is metadata table complete and validated? (Yes/No/Unknown)
      □ Is decoder validation interface staged? (Yes/No/Unknown)
    If any are NO or UNKNOWN: COMPLETE THEM BEFORE committing to path choice in turn 191
    This is low-cost insurance against forced pivots

(4) PARSING SCRIPT PREPARATION:
    Before Q196 and Q200 execute (turn 190), prepare parsing script for baseline_run1_batched outputs (task b583n243e):
    - Script should (1) check output directory structure, (2) confirm Tensor schema match, (3) error loudly if format mismatch
    - Have script ready to execute immediately when task b583n243e completes
    - This prevents silent parsing failures during turn 190 analysis

TURN 190 (MAIN ANALYSIS): EXECUTE Q196-Q202

ASSUMING all four streams have completed (or status is clear by turn 190):

(1) Q196 ANALYSIS: Baseline_run1 Batched Execution
    IF b583n243e outputs available and parse successful:
      → Extract Tensor list (6 cycles, batch_size=5)
      → Validate: covers ~30 turns? strand_stability_analysis present?
      → STATUS: EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL
    IF b583n243e outputs missing or parse fails:
      → DECLARE FAILURE: 'baseline_run1 batched (b583n243e) [timeout/error/format mismatch]'
      → CONSEQUENCE: Same-conversation per-turn vs. batched comparison unavailable
      → MITIGATION: Proceed with archive analysis (baseline_run2, compact_trim) if they complete

(2) Q197-Q198 ANALYSIS: Archive Projections Status
    IF baseline_run2 (b5mvz85yy) outputs available:
      → Parse Tensor cycles for 100-turn session
      → Extract epistemic trajectory (truth, indeterminacy, falsity per cycle)
      → STATUS: READY FOR Q199 (N=15 assumption test)
    IF baseline_run2 outputs missing/timeout:
      → DECLARE TIMEOUT explicitly
      → CONSEQUENCE: Cannot test N=15 assumption on full session
      → DECISION: Does archive analysis proceed on compact_trim alone, or is data too sparse?
    
    IF long_compact_trim (bnk191til) outputs available:
      → Parse Tensor cycles for 40-turn session
      → Extract epistemic trajectory
      → STATUS: READY FOR Q201 (cross-variant comparison)
    IF long_compact_trim outputs missing/timeout:
      → DECLARE TIMEOUT explicitly
      → CONSEQUENCE: Cannot compare baseline vs. compact_trim variant effect
      → DECISION: Can H2 hypothesis be tested without variant comparison? (probably NO)

(3) Q199 ANALYSIS: N=15 Assumption Test (Contingent on Q197 success)
    Extract from baseline_run2 epistemic trajectory:
    - Truth values: t1, t3, t5, t7, t10, t15
    - Indeterminacy values: i1, i3, i5, i7, i10, i15
    - Falsity values: f1, f3, f5, f7, f10, f15
    
    Examine pattern:
    - Plot or tabulate: Do values show STABLE trend or HIGH VARIANCE?
    - Define stability: standard_deviation of values / mean < 0.2 = STABLE; > 0.2 = NOISY
    - EXPLICIT FINDING: 'N=15 assumption [HELD/FAILED]. Trend [CLEAR/WEAK/NOISY]. Pattern clarity: [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW].'
    
    Interpretation:
    - HELD + HIGH CLARITY: N=15 sampling is safe for other runs. Can extrapolate patterns from short samples.
    - HELD + MEDIUM CLARITY: N=15 sampling is acceptable but noisy. Findings should be labeled PROVISIONAL.
    - FAILED + NOISY: N=15 assumption broken. Must project longer samples for pattern confidence. Archive methodology question.
    
    OUTPUT by end turn 190: Explicit statement of assumption status and pattern clarity

(4) Q200 ANALYSIS: Per-Turn vs. Batched Convergence (Contingent on Q196 success)
    Comparison: baseline_run1 per-turn (from cycle 62, 10 cycles, ~50 turns) vs. baseline_run1 batched (b583n243e, 6 cycles, ~30 turns)
    
    For overlapping 30 turns:
    Metric 1 - Truth trajectory:
      Extract truth values from cycles 1-6 of per-turn
      Extract truth values from cycles 1-6 of batched
      Compare: |truth_per_turn - truth_batched| per cycle
      Convergence test: mean absolute difference < 0.1 (truth scale 0-1) = CONVERGED
    
    Metric 2 - Indeterminacy trajectory: same method
    Metric 3 - Falsity trajectory: same method
    Metric 4 - Strand count: extract strand count per cycle from both. Do they track together?
    
    EXPLICIT FINDING:
    'Per-turn and batched projections [CONVERGE/DIVERGE] on baseline_run1 first 30 turns.'
      - If CONVERGE: 'Mean epistemic difference: ΔTruth=[X], ΔIndeterm=[Y], ΔFalsity=[Z]. Conclusion: projector is mode-insensitive.'
      - If DIVERGE: 'Mean epistemic difference: ΔTruth=[X], ΔIndeterm=[Y], ΔFalsity=[Z]. HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY: turn-grouping affects epistemic patterns. Mechanism investigation required.'
    
    OUTPUT by end turn 190: Explicit convergence/divergence assessment with magnitudes

(5) Q201 ANALYSIS: Cross-Variant Comparison (Contingent on Q197 and Q198 success)
    Comparison: baseline_run2 (100 turns) vs. long_compact_trim (40 turns)
    NOTE: Sessions are different lengths. Compare like-with-like: use first 40 turns of baseline_run2 against full long_compact_trim.
    
    For first 40 turns:
    Metric 1 - Truth trajectory:
      Extract truth values from baseline_run2 turns 1-40
      Extract truth values from long_compact_trim (all turns)
      Compare per-cycle: baseline_truth vs. trim_truth
      Primary finding: Does baseline show higher truth (positive ΔTruth) or lower truth (negative ΔTruth)?
    
    Metric 2 - Indeterminacy trajectory
    Metric 3 - Falsity trajectory
    Metric 4 - Strand count and meta/domain ratio
    Metric 5 - Declared losses: does trim accumulate MORE losses (evidence of degradation)?
    
    EXPLICIT FINDING:
    'Cross-variant comparison (baseline_run2 vs. long_compact_trim, first 40 turns):'
      - 'Mean ΔTruth = baseline_truth - trim_truth = [+X or -X]'
      - 'If ΔTruth > +0.1 (baseline significantly stronger): Preliminary H2 finding: meta-recursion essential. Compact_trim shows epistemic degradation.'
      - 'If ΔTruth ≈ 0 (equivalent): Preliminary H2 finding: meta-recursion effect minimal or neutral.'
      - 'If ΔTruth < -0.1 (trim significantly stronger): Unexpected. Meta-recursion removal may improve epistemic clarity?'
      - LABEL ALL FINDINGS: 'This is [PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY] until decoder closure validation (Q202) completes.'
    
    OUTPUT by end turn 190: Explicit ΔTruth assessment with preliminary H2 interpretation, confidence label

(6) IMPLICIT LOOP BACK TO USER (End of Turn 190):
    After Q196-Q201 analysis complete, synthesis:
    'Here's what the parallel streams show: [summary of Q196-Q201 findings]. Same-conversation comparison [converged/diverged]. Variant comparison shows ΔTruth=[X], suggesting [H2 interpretation]. All findings remain [PROVISIONAL] pending decoder closure validation. Awaiting test_author decoder results (Q202).'
    
    This keeps user in loop and surfaces findings BEFORE decoder validation locks them in as CONFIDENT

TURN 191 (DECODER VALIDATION + FALLBACK DECISION): EXECUTE Q202-Q203

(1) Q202 ANALYSIS: Decoder Closure Status
    IF test_author_run1 projection outputs available:
      → Have outputs been dispatched to external model decoder interface?
      → Run decoder validation: Can external model comprehend baseline tensors?
      → Measure: comprehension_score = % of baseline tensor fields successfully decoded
      → Threshold check: comprehension_score > 75%? (YES = baseline HIGH QUALITY, NO = baseline SUSPECT)
      → If compact_trim was analyzed: also test decoder on trim variant. Does decoder handle trim as well as baseline?
      
      EXPLICIT FINDING:
        'Decoder validation results:'
        '- Baseline tensors: [X]% comprehension [PASSED/FAILED]'
        '- Compact_trim tensors (if tested): [Y]% comprehension [PASSED/FAILED]'
        'Interpretation: Baseline tensor quality is [HIGH/SUSPECT]. H2 hypothesis testing can proceed with [CONFIDENT/EXPLORATORY] confidence.'
    
    IF test_author_run1 outputs missing/decoder validation not yet run:
      → QUEUE immediately if not already done
      → Note: Decoder validation is blocking final H2 confidence assignment
      → All H2 findings remain PROVISIONAL until decoder validates
      → Expected completion turn 191-192

(2) Q203 ANALYSIS: Fallback Path Clarity
    Synthesize outcomes of Q196-Q202:
    
    GREEN GATE (All systems go):
    ✓ Q196 baseline_run1 batched completed successfully
    ✓ Q197 baseline_run2 per-turn completed successfully
    ✓ Q198 long_compact_trim per-turn completed successfully
    ✓ Q199 N=15 assumption HOLDS (pattern clear)
    ✓ Q200 Per-turn and batched CONVERGE (mode-insensitive)
    ✓ Q201 Cross-variant comparison clear (ΔTruth sign and magnitude confident)
    ✓ Q202 Decoder validation PASSES (baseline quality HIGH)
    → DECISION: Proceed with FRONT PATH. H2 hypothesis testing is CONFIDENT.
    → CYCLE 64 PROTOCOL: Finalize H2 hypothesis assessment. Test H1, H3, H4 hypotheses. Conduct full retrospective analysis.
    → TIMELINE: Cycles 64-65 hypothesis testing and analysis; cycle 66 design decision

    YELLOW GATE (Proceed with caveats):
    ◐ Q196 baseline_run1 batched [timeout/fails] — same-conversation mode comparison unavailable
    ◐ Q199 N=15 assumption HELD but pattern noisy — findings should be labeled PROVISIONAL
    ◐ Q200 Per-turn and batched DIVERGE significantly — HIGH-VALUE DISCOVERY but adds uncertainty
    ◐ Q202 Decoder validation PASSES but with low comprehension (e.g., 76%) — baseline barely acceptable
    → DECISION: Proceed with FRONT PATH but downgrade confidence to PROVISIONAL.
    → CYCLE 64 PROTOCOL: Conduct archive analysis with PROVISIONAL labels. Highlight mode-sensitivity or decoder boundary issues. Prepare prospective validation for cycle 65 to confirm findings.
    → TIMELINE: Cycles 64-65 analysis + prospective design; cycle 65-66 prospective validation; cycle 67 design decision (extended timeline)

    RED GATE (Archive path infeasible):
    ✗ Q197 baseline_run2 per-turn [timeout/fails] — archive analysis impossible
    ✗ Q198 long_compact_trim per-turn [timeout/fails] — variant comparison impossible
    ✗ Q199 N=15 assumption FAILS — epistemic pattern unclear even at N=15, suggests projector instability
    ✗ Q200 Per-turn and batched diverge, but both streams fail — mode comparison indeterminate
    ✗ Q202 Decoder validation FAILS — baseline tensor quality suspect
    → DECISION: Archive path is infeasible. Pivot to FALLBACK B (fresh prospective experiments).
    → CYCLE 64 PROTOCOL: Finalize prospective experiment design (baseline vs. compact_trim vs. optimized). Single input, single projector version, controlled execution.
    → TIMELINE: Cycles 64-65 prospective design finalization; cycles 65-67 execution; cycle 68 analysis (significantly extended, but cleaner data)

    EXPLICIT GATE OUTPUT (Turn 191, binding decision):
    Format: '[GREEN/YELLOW/RED] GATE: [Path] is VIABLE. Rationale: [Q196-Q202 outcomes]. CYCLE 64 PROTOCOL: [specific next-step actions].'

(3) CONTINGENCY PROTOCOL ACTIVATION (Turn 191, conditional):
    
    IF GREEN GATE:
      → Queue additional archive runs (test_author variants, other baseline runs if available)
      → Prepare hypothesis testing framework (H1-H4 definitions, comparison matrix, statistical tests)
      → Expected cycle 64 start: hypothesis testing execution
    
    IF YELLOW GATE:
      → Mark all findings as PROVISIONAL in output
      → Design targeted prospective validation for cycle 65 (test specific hypothesis that archive analysis flagged)
      → Prepare contingency: if prospective validation contradicts archive findings, archive findings downgraded to EXPLORATORY
    
    IF RED GATE:
      → ACTIVATE FALLBACK B PROTOCOL
      → Finalize prospective experiment design immediately (turns 191-192)
      → Specification: baseline vs. compact_trim vs. [optimized variant]
      → Submit design for approval by cycle 64 start
      → Queue execution for cycles 65-67

TURN 192 (SYNTHESIS AND CYCLE 64 HANDOFF):

Cycle 63 deliverables to cycle 64:

  1. PROJECTION EXECUTION STATUS: All four streams (Q196-Q198) completion status explicitly reported
  2. SAME-CONVERSATION VALIDATION: Q200 per-turn vs. batched convergence assessment. If divergent, mechanism investigation.
  3. ASSUMPTION TEST RESULTS: Q199 N=15 assumption [HELD/FAILED] with pattern clarity score
  4. CROSS-VARIANT ANALYSIS: Q201 ΔTruth comparison (baseline vs. compact_trim) with preliminary H2 interpretation
  5. DECODER CLOSURE OUTCOME: Q202 decoder validation results (baseline quality assessment)
  6. FALLBACK PATH DECISION: Q203 explicit [GREEN/YELLOW/RED] gate with rationale
  7. CYCLE 64 PROTOCOL: Clear next-step instructions based on chosen path
  8. CONFIDENCE LABELING: All findings explicitly labeled [CONFIDENT/PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY] with data basis
  9. DECLARED LOSSES: All unresolved information gaps or failed assumptions
 10. USER INPUT INTEGRATION: Document whether Q209 external input reshaped analysis

If cycle 63 delivers all ten items by turn 192 with explicit reasoning, the path forward is LOCKED IN and cycle 64 can execute with full clarity.

=== CRITICAL DISCIPLINE FOR CYCLES 63-64 ===

1. USER INPUT INTEGRATION IS NOT OPTIONAL: If user provides observation in turn 189, INTEGRATE immediately before turn 190 analysis. Do not execute analysis in parallel with user input.

2. FALLBACK READINESS IS INSURANCE: Confirm FALLBACK B design, metadata, decoder interface exist before committing to path. Low cost; prevents surprised forced pivots.

3. PARSING ROBUSTNESS: Prepare error-checking script for baseline_run1_batched outputs. Silent parsing failures are enemy. Script should FAIL LOUDLY if format mismatch.

4. GATE DECISION IS BINDING: Once turn 191 determines GREEN/YELLOW/RED, cycle 64 COMMITS to that path. No flip-flopping. If data suggests path change by cycle 64, cycle 64 re-evaluates, but within-cycle reversals are prohibited.

5. DECODER CLOSURE IS PRE-REQUISITE: Do not assign CONFIDENT confidence to H2 findings if decoder validation is pending or failed. All findings remain PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY until decoder validates.

6. OUTCOME NEUTRALITY: Analysis will follow data. If compact_trim is stronger, say so. If weaker, say so. If equivalent, say so. Do not force H2 into preferred direction.

=== SUCCESS CRITERIA FOR CYCLE 63 ===

Cycle 63 is successful if it delivers (by turn 192):
  1. PROJECTION STATUS: Q196-Q198 completion explicitly reported (all four streams or failure declarations)
  2. SAME-CONVERSATION VALIDATION: Q200 convergence/divergence explicitly assessed

  4. VARIANT COMPARISON: Q201 ΔTruth calculated with preliminary H2 interpretation
  5. DECODER CLOSURE: Q202 validation results explicit (baseline quality HIGH/SUSPECT)
  6. FALLBACK DECISION: Q203 explicit [GREEN/YELLOW/RED] gate with blockers or validation status
  7. CYCLE 64 PROTOCOL: Clear next-step instructions contingent on chosen path
  8. CONFIDENCE LABELING: All findings labeled [CONFIDENT/PROVISIONAL/EXPLORATORY] with rationale
  9. DECLARED LOSSES: All incomplete or failed analyses explicitly listed
  10. USER INTEGRATION: Document whether external input reshaped cycle 63 (Q209)

If cycle 63 delivers all ten items by turn 192 with explicit reasoning, outcome uncertainty is resolved, path forward is clear, and cycle 64 can execute with confidence."
